# Routeveil

> Routeveil is an open-source React and TypeScript transition engine for React Router. It provides typed, per-navigation page animations and full-screen overlay transitions while keeping transition logic out of route components.

## Official resources

- [Website](https://www.routeveil.dev/): Project overview.
- [Documentation](https://www.routeveil.dev/docs): Installation, quick start, API, transitions, options, and reduced-motion behavior.
- [Interactive laboratory](https://www.routeveil.dev/lab): Built-in page and overlay transition demonstrations.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/milkevich/routeveil): Source code, issues, and README.
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/routeveil): Published package metadata and versions.
- [Complete AI-readable reference](https://www.routeveil.dev/llms-full.txt): Expanded product and API summary.

## Package usage

- Install package: `npm install routeveil`
- Public import entry: `routeveil/react-router`
- React peer range: `^18.0.0 || ^19.0.0`.
- React DOM peer range: `^18.0.0 || ^19.0.0`.
- React Router DOM peer range: `^6.27.0 || ^7.0.0`.
- Package format: ES module with included TypeScript declarations.
- License: MIT.

## Compatibility

Exact tested combinations:

- React 18.0.0, React DOM 18.0.0, React Router DOM 6.27.0.
- React 18.3.1, React DOM 18.3.1, React Router DOM 6.30.4.
- React 19.0.0, React DOM 19.0.0, React Router DOM 6.27.0.
- React 19.2.8, React DOM 19.2.8, React Router DOM 6.30.4.
- React 18.0.0, React DOM 18.0.0, React Router DOM 7.0.0.
- React 18.3.1, React DOM 18.3.1, React Router DOM 7.18.1.
- React 19.0.0, React DOM 19.0.0, React Router DOM 7.0.0.
- React 19.2.8, React DOM 19.2.8, React Router DOM 7.18.1.
- React 19.2.7, React DOM 19.2.7, React Router DOM 7.18.1, matching the repository lockfile.

React Router 5, React Router 8, React Router DOM versions below 6.27.0, and untested combinations are unsupported. The public import remains `routeveil/react-router`.

## Core API

- `RouteveilProvider`: Coordinates transitions and navigation commits.
- `RouteveilView`: Marks the routed region used by page transitions.
- `RouteveilLink`: Adds an optional typed transition to React Router links.
- `useRouteveilNavigate`: Performs transition-aware programmatic navigation.
- `useRouteveilTransition`: Plays a transition without changing the route.

## Interrupted navigation

- Routeveil runs one active transition and exposes one active promise at a time.
- Additional Routeveil links, navigation-hook calls, and playback requests receive the active promise without queueing or committing another destination.
- Browser Back and Forward, ordinary React Router navigation, plain links, and direct history changes cancel current visual work and leave the latest external location in control.
- Successful and external route changes preserve meaningful application focus or focus the incoming `RouteveilView` with `preventScroll`; playback and failed unchanged navigation restore prior focus when appropriate.
- Every run cleans up its animations, inert state, temporary attributes, overlays, timers, location waiters, and transition phase.
- Routeveil does not currently support cancellation and replacement, request queues, or last-request-wins concurrency.

The website, documentation, GitHub repository, and npm page above are the canonical public sources for Routeveil.
